description_long: This is a traditional wooden Chinese junk boat featuring a long, narrow hull constructed from planks of brown timber. The vessel is equipped with three prominent, battened red sails, each mounted on a separate mast, with the largest sail at the center and two smaller sails fore and aft. The deck is open and spacious, with visible wooden planking and a raised stern section. The hull sides are reinforced with dark horizontal bands, and the bow is slightly upturned, giving the boat a distinctive profile. The rigging and masts are made of dark wood or bamboo, and the sails are divided into panels by horizontal battens. There are no visible modern features such as motors or electronics, emphasizing its historical and traditional design.
description: A traditional wooden Chinese junk boat with three red battened sails and a long, narrow brown timber hull. The open deck and distinctive upturned bow highlight its classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed bow, central mast, and the main red sail aligned with the hull.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the hull, all three masts with red sails, and the raised stern.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the stern, the aft mast and sail, and the open deck layout.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the hull's curvature, the three sails, and the detailed wooden planking.
